admission_rule.state_space: finite-dimensional effectively presented compact rational box in R^n
admission_rule.time_domain: nonnegative real time over a declared compact horizon
admission_rule.dynamics: autonomous or input-indexed vector field with an explicit computable global Lipschitz bound on the admitted domain
admission_rule.inputs: piecewise-rational controls with finite descriptions and declared switching times
admission_rule.events: finite effectively decidable guard family with isolated crossings and declared reset maps
admission_rule.reasoning_layer: finite commitments, grounds, dependencies, revisions, and evidential annotations indexed to continuous trajectories or event history
admission_rule.presentation: algorithms for evaluating vector fields, guards, resets, and certified rational error bounds
exclusions: non-Lipschitz dynamics without a declared solution-selection rule
unbounded horizons without an effective continuation argument

observation_boundary.analyst_access: full frozen mathematical presentation and certified bounds
observation_boundary.represented_agent_access: only source-declared observations, commitments, and history
prohibited: finite time grid treated as the exact trajectory

nonclaims: all continuous reasoning systems are admitted
all differential equations have unique computable flows
certified approximation is exact physical correspondence
continuous dynamics establishes a universal structure
necessity minimality or uniqueness is established
